meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn to build a professional SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your career as an AI Builder.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop building toy apps and start shipping production-ready SaaS starter kits. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you create modular, scalable systems that demonstrate architectural maturity, turning your GitHub from a graveyard into a high-converting career asset.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor of a blank IDE, feeling the weight of the 'no experience' label crushing your job prospects. You’ve spent months following tutorials that lead to nowhere, leaving you with a resume of generic clones that fail to impress a single hiring manager. The industry doesn't care about your coursework; they care about your ability to ship.

It is time to pivot from student to engineer. By building a robust SaaS starter kit, you aren't just writing code—you are architecting a business foundation. This tutorial bridges the gap between theory and execution, using the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code to help you build a professional-grade product that proves you can solve real-world problems at scale.

steps.title: Define Your SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation. We focus on authentication, database schema, and payment integration to ensure your kit is production-ready, not just a prototype.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean SaaS starter kit architecture using Next.js 14, Supabase for auth/DB, and Stripe for payments. Include a modular component library structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project with Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe integrations'

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Data integrity is the hallmark of a senior developer. We configure automated migrations to ensure your app scales without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create SQL migration scripts for a multi-tenant SaaS user table, subscription status, and project workspace schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate and apply SQL migrations for multi-tenant SaaS schema'

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Security is non-negotiable. We build a middleware-based RBAC system to protect sensitive user endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware for Next.js that enforces RBAC, differentiating between 'admin', 'editor', and 'viewer' roles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement Next.js middleware for role-based access control'

steps.title: Integrate AI-Powered Features
steps.description: Add a competitive edge by integrating LLM capabilities directly into your kit, making it an 'AI-first' application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a reusable AI service module using the Vercel AI SDK to stream responses from OpenAI or Anthropic models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add Vercel AI SDK integration for streaming chat responses'

steps.title: Deploy to Global Edge
steps.description: A portfolio project is useless if it's not live. We configure CI/CD for instant deployment to Vercel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel on push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure GitHub Actions for automated deployment'

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from day one; it differentiates a hobby project from a professional SaaS.
Use 'feature flags' to toggle experimental features, showing recruiters you understand production release management.
Document your architectural trade-offs in a README.md; explain why you chose PostgreSQL over NoSQL.
Optimize your database queries for N+1 problems early; performance is the first thing senior engineers check.
Include a 'test coverage' metric in your repo; high test coverage is the single best signal of a disciplined developer.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, create a 'case study' video explaining one complex technical challenge you solved during the build, then pin it to your GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your lack of experience define your potential. You have the tools, the roadmap, and the AI-powered leverage to build a product that commands respect. The industry is waiting for the next generation of builders—are you ready to step up? Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
